issues.body:
Wire Pip's picked primaries from merged PR #754 (verdict map in its comments):

Now (this issue): promote office_wide_day as the main.tscn gameplay backdrop (dim/low-contrast, must not fight UI legibility -- add a darkening overlay if needed) and records_microfiche as the leaderboard screen ground (first concrete step of the institutional-records direction from #743's TODO). Promote remaining keepers to godot/assets staged-but-unwired.

With #755 (event dialog theming): event-genre art map -- crisis=V1, opportunity=V2, board=V2, secret=V3; secondaries staged.

Later (DQ-35, not this issue): variant rotation, achievement unlocks (crisis V3, records_vault), conditional selection (opportunity V4 by success likelihood), records_trophy_terminal as post-game achievements surface.

Size: S-M.

issues.title: art: app-icon candidates + settings-bg alternatives (fast pass)
issues.body:
Fast-pass **stopgap** candidate set ahead of the afternoon designer hire -- quantity + variety over polish. Do not merge; this is for Pip's fast-pass iteration.

## Review here first
**Contact sheet: `tools/art_review/icon_pass_2026-07-21.html`** (open locally; relative image paths, click any icon for the 1024px master, small pair under each icon is the 32/48px render).

## Deliverable 1 -- app-icon candidates (15)
For the Windows-export application icon (SmartScreen fix, issue #732) + logo stopgap. All in the locked palette (`docs/art/PALETTE_AND_DOOM_INTENSITY.md`), rendered 32 -> 1024px so 32x32 legibility is checkable directly.
- **Office cat (3):** `cat_face`, `cat_face_doom` (soured-red worried variant), `cat_sitting`
- **Doom gauge/dial (4):** `dial_clock` (doomsday-clock), `dial_half`, `dial_ring`, `dial_radar`
- **Bureaucratic seal/stamp (4):** `seal_star`, `seal_hourglass` ("PAPERWORK THAT MIGHT / SAVE THE WORLD"), `seal_check`, `seal_red`
- **P(Doom) typographic (4):** `type_wordmark`, `type_amber`, `type_mono` (best at 32px), `type_dial_paren` (gauge inside the parens)

## Deliverable 2 -- settings background, 3 warmer alternatives (512x512)
Drop-in replacements for the sage/green `tex_painted_metal_panel_512` (the greenest kept ground), same peel + corner/edge-screw composition shifted warm: `warm_ochre`, `warm_terracotta` (warmest), `warm_bakelite` (cozy deep brown).

## Pipeline note / blocker
Intended pipeline was in-repo **gpt-image-1** (`tools/assets`), but **`OPENAI_API_KEY` was absent in every env scope** (no key file, no GEMINI key either), so it could not run. To still deliver real PNGs today without burning the parallel agent's pixellab credits on off-register pixel-art, these were generated **procedurally** (PIL/numpy, no API): `tools/art_review/gen_icon_candidates.py`, `tools/art_review/gen_settings_grounds.py`. Set `OPENAI_API_KEY` to run higher-fidelity gpt-image-1 versions (esp. the office cat, which benefits most from a generative model).

Spend: **$0.00**.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.body: From Pip's #749 visual pass: event popup buttons are still grey. Expected -- event_dialog.gd draws a code-styled panel and its buttons were outside #749's nine themed menu scenes. Apply menu_theme (or an event-specific variation) to event dialogs and response windows so popups match the themed chrome; coordinate with the event-genre hero art from PR #754 (the dialogs are about to get backgrounds, so theme buttons + hero art wiring may be one lane). Size: S.

issues.title: Dev overlays (DEV MODE + F3) can't read live game state; 29 runtime errors; nudge/advance-turn wiring
issues.body: Playtest 85ad582: DEV MODE (backslash) and F3 both report **'no active game state'** during a live game, so risk-pools + rival-labs sections are empty. F3 also shows **29 runtime errors** (all 'color white refcounted ...') + a perf tab (RAM/FPS) but no risk pools. DEV MODE resource +/- **nudge buttons may not be wired** to change state (verify). **Advance-turn** gives no visible feedback (works? overlay may cover the UI). Also: **backslash should CLOSE** dev mode (toggle). Design guard needed: dev-mode state pokes should NOT corrupt the verification/event log.

issues.title: chore: docs typographic ASCII cleanup (em-dashes, box-drawing) -- low priority
issues.body:
Follow-up from PR #751 (blocking no-emoji hook + godot/ ASCII purge). Remaining out of scope there: ~1282 em-dashes and box-drawing architecture diagrams across ~79 docs/ files. Convert to ASCII (em-dash -> --, box-drawing -> |/+/- ASCII trees) in one mechanical PR, then consider extending the no-emoji hook's full-ASCII coverage to docs/ (excluding docs/archive/ and docs/game-design/WORKSHOP_2_BACKLOG.md, which remain exempt historical files unless separately ruled).

Ruled low priority by Pip 2026-07-21; build in a quiet window OUTSIDE active-lane sessions -- mass mechanical edits across docs conflict with in-flight doc lanes, so schedule when no doc-touching lanes are running.

Size: S (mechanical, scripted).

issues.title: diag+harden: leaderboard crash diagnostic builds + build-verify/scene-smoke/asset-validation defenses
issues.body:
## Two jobs: (A) prepare to NAIL the release-only leaderboard segfault, (B) bulletproof against the class.

**The bug:** RELEASE-ONLY segfault while LOADING the leaderboard scene (game-over -> leaderboard), BEFORE `_ready` runs. Headless cannot reproduce it (verified: the new smoke test add-to-trees + runs `_ready` on the leaderboard scene cleanly). So this PR ships diagnostic builds + protocol + defenses, not a blind fix. **Do not merge** -- Pip runs the builds first.

### Part A -- the kill kit
- **3 verified-fresh diagnostic builds** produced by `tools/build_release.py` (each proven built-from-current-source; see below). Placed at stable paths under `builds/leaderboard-diag/{debug01,strip01,min01}/` (gitignored).
- `docs/LEADERBOARD_CRASH_DIAGNOSIS.md` -- **committed** ordered kill protocol (which build, exact `--verbose` command, what each result means).
- `leaderboard_screen.gd` -- `[LBTRACE-n]` stderr probes bracketing every `_ready` step.
- `leaderboard_screen_minimal.tscn` + `leaderboard_screen_min_stub.gd` -- reproducible minimal-scene fixture for the structure-vs-content bisection.
- `theme_manager.gd` -- drop the two dead `tex_cyan_ispf`/`tex_oxidized_copper` string refs (nothing `load()`s them).

### Part B -- bulletproofing (half the value)
- **`tools/build_release.py`** -- exports from a clean state (`rm -rf .godot`) then PROVES the pack is fresh via a unique **marker filename** (key finding: script string literals do NOT survive as grep-able text in this project's binary-tokenized `.gdc` export -- the old "grep for a printerr tag" advice is unreliable; filenames in the pack file table do survive). No build ships unverified.
- **`test_ui_scene_ready_smoke.gd`** -- add-to-tree + run `_ready` on every `scenes/ui` scene + standalone screen (the existing instantiate-only smoke never ran `_ready`). 21 scenes exercised.
- **`tools/validate_assets.py`** + `validate_assets_probe.gd` -- load every asset under `godot/assets`, fail loud on any that will not resolve.
- **`docs/POSTMORTEM_v0.11.0_ALPHA.md`** -- stale-export-cache trap, verify-the-pack discipline, the `all_resources` packing gotcha, and the headless render-gate limitation.

### Gate
Fast gate green: **534 tests, 0 failures (60/60 files)**. `build_release.py` dogfooded 3x (all PASS); `validate_assets.py` PASS.

### Best current hypothesis
Textures are almost certainly NOT the cause (dead string refs; welcome.tscn loads REAL textures fine as the boot scene). Leading suspect: the **`OptionButton` (SeedDropdown)** -- the one node type unique to this scene, which builds an internal PopupMenu/Window at instantiation, a plausible pre-`_ready` native-crash site in the release renderer. Build 3 (no OptionButton) is the direct test.

Headless cannot reproduce this class of crash; the human release-build playtest remains the final gate.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: Art tooling: compare-and-contrast mode for kept assets (batch style reviews)
issues.body:
Pip 2026-07-21, from the round-2 sweep review: the hide-on-verdict viewer flow made clearing hundreds of cells easy ("way way way easier... I could now clear hundreds without getting tired"). Next capability: when doing batch STYLE reviews, an easy compare-and-contrast view of already-CHOSEN (kept) assets -- side-by-side across styles/palettes for refinement rounds, not just triage of new generations.

This edges toward a lightweight art-asset management layer (a CMS-ish view over art_source + verdicts.json). Keep it repo-resident HTML like the existing viewer (tools/art_review/build.py pattern), not an external service.

Not scheduled -- backlog until the next big style-refinement round makes it worth building.

issues.title: Check Godot port for team_player uncapped-stacking exploit + poaching rate (salvaged from pre-Godot notes)

milestone.title: v0.13 -- Rivals and News
milestone.description: Rivals M-tier surfaces (panel, race chart, poaching rework), News channel v1 (DQ-32), voice re-skin of generic event content, tutorial mode, DQ-22 ADR workshop.

issues.body:
**Salvaged from the pre-Godot PERSONNEL_BALANCING_NOTES.md during the 2026-07-16 archival sweep.** Two personnel-balance concerns from the pygame era — verify whether they survived into the Godot port, fix if present. For the QA/bug-shakedown phase, not a blocker.

1. **team_player uncapped-stacking (potential exploit).** Old notes flagged a team_player trait whose synergy bonus stacked without a cap — N team-players compounding into a runaway surplus. By our exploit definition (engine misbehaviour, not clever strategy — DESIGN_PHILOSOPHY), an uncapped stack is a bug to get ahead of. Action: grep Godot researcher/trait code for additive-per-teammate synergy without a cap; if present, cap it + add a test. If traits are not ported yet, note so it is designed-out (ties DQ-15).

2. **Poaching rate.** Old notes had rival poaching ~4%/turn; Pip's target is roughly once a year (ADR-0011 / DQ-22 aggressive-hiring). Reconcile the current cadence to the once-a-year intuition (re-denominate like the other per-tick artifacts calibration caught).

Both feed DQ-15 (researcher archetypes) and DQ-22 (world-shoots-back midgame).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: Cross-repo sync: fold player-guide mapping into the #545 cleanup pass

milestone.title: v0.13 -- Rivals and News
milestone.description: Rivals M-tier surfaces (panel, race chart, poaching rework), News channel v1 (DQ-32), voice re-skin of generic event content, tutorial mode, DQ-22 ADR workshop.

issues.body:
Context: #545 rewrites docs/sync-config.yml and sync-documentation.yml, removing dead no-op mappings. Today only docs/shared/* flows, and only into siblings' docs/ (dev-visible, not public/).

This issue: while #545 edits those files, add one real live mapping for the player guide.

Acceptance criteria:
- Mapping: SSOT player guide -> website public/ (player-visible), per the website-publish issue.
- Verify with a trivial change that the website receives it (no reintroduced dead mapping).
- Token choice (CROSS_REPO_TOKEN vs WEBSITE_SYNC_TOKEN) consistent with #545's consolidation decision.

Pointers: docs/sync-config.yml, .github/workflows/sync-documentation.yml, .github/workflows/sync-dev-blog.yml, issue #545. Size: S.

issues.title: Website: publish the player guide to pdoom1.com with pdoom1 -> website sync

milestone.title: v0.13 -- Rivals and News
milestone.description: Rivals M-tier surfaces (panel, race chart, poaching rework), News channel v1 (DQ-32), voice re-skin of generic event content, tutorial mode, DQ-22 ADR workshop.

issues.body:
Context: pdoom1-website is static (netlify publishes public/). public/docs/ is developer-facing; there is no player how-to-play page and nothing renders the game's PLAYERGUIDE. A proven push-to-public pattern exists: .github/workflows/sync-dev-blog.yml pushes into website public/blog/ via WEBSITE_SYNC_TOKEN.

Acceptance criteria:
- Player-facing How to Play page under website public/ (e.g. public/docs/how-to-play), sourced from the game repo PLAYERGUIDE, with a nav link.
- Game-repo workflow modeled on sync-dev-blog.yml (NOT the leaderboard PHP path) pushing rendered output on change.
- No hand-maintained copy in the website repo.

Pointers: docs/PLAYERGUIDE.md, .github/workflows/sync-dev-blog.yml, website netlify.toml, website public/docs/. Coordinate with #545. Size: M (Phase 4).

issues.title: Onboarding: opt-in Tutorial Mode (relaunchable guided steps)

milestone.title: v0.13 -- Rivals and News
milestone.description: Rivals M-tier surfaces (panel, race chart, poaching rework), News channel v1 (DQ-32), voice re-skin of generic event content, tutorial mode, DQ-22 ADR workshop.

issues.body:
Context: no tutorial engine exists in godot/ (verified). Builds on the first-launch skeleton and hint data.

Acceptance criteria:
- TutorialManager autoload driving an ordered step overlay (highlight target, instruction, advance on action or Next).
- Offered on first launch; relaunchable via a Tutorial entry on the welcome menu.
- tutorial_completed persisted in GameConfig; never forced on returning players.
- Cover the six PLAYERGUIDE areas (resources, actions, attention, turn management, events/milestones, upgrades).

Pointers: godot/autoload/, godot/scripts/ui/welcome_screen.gd, godot/scripts/ui/main_ui.gd, docs/PLAYERGUIDE.md. Depends on the skeleton + hints issues. Size: L (Phase 3).

issues.title: Onboarding: data-driven contextual hints (Factorio-style show-once triggers)

milestone.title: v0.12 -- First Contact
milestone.description: Public alpha readiness (distribution channel decision owed -- see docs/ROADMAP.md): first-contact UX fixes, onboarding phases 0-2, export metadata, share loop, leaderboard hardening, monthly league v0. Capacity basis: ~1-2 effort-days/week.

issues.body:
Context: docs/PLAYERGUIDE.md already names four first-time triggers (first hire, first upgrade, out of Action Points/Attention, high doom). None implemented. Game is data-driven (godot/data/*.json); hints should be data, not hardcoded.

Acceptance criteria:
- godot/data/help/hints.json: entries {id, title, body, trigger, guide_anchor}.
- HintManager autoload firing each hint once, gated by GameConfig.show_hints, shown ids persisted.
- Wire the four PLAYERGUIDE triggers.
- Unit test: every guide_anchor resolves to a real PLAYERGUIDE section (fast gate).

Pointers: docs/PLAYERGUIDE.md, godot/data/, godot/autoload/, godot/scripts/ui/main_ui.gd. Depends on the first-launch skeleton issue. Size: M (Phase 2, highest gameplay ROI).

issues.title: Onboarding: first-launch help layer skeleton (settings flag + dismissible overlay)

milestone.title: v0.12 -- First Contact
milestone.description: Public alpha readiness (distribution channel decision owed -- see docs/ROADMAP.md): first-contact UX fixes, onboarding phases 0-2, export metadata, share loop, leaderboard hardening, monthly league v0. Capacity basis: ~1-2 effort-days/week.

issues.body:
Context: the only in-play onboarding is getting_started_hint in godot/scripts/ui/main_ui.gd (visible turn < 3). godot/autoload/game_config.gd already has games_played (free first-launch detector) and last_seen_version (proven show-once gate, see whats_new_modal.gd).

Acceptance criteria:
- Add show_hints: bool = true to GameConfig, persisted, exposed as a "Show gameplay hints" settings toggle.
- On first launch (games_played == 0) show one dismissible welcome/help overlay pointing at the Player Guide.
- Respect the toggle everywhere hints show.
- Reuse the last_seen_version show-once pattern.

Pointers: godot/autoload/game_config.gd, godot/scripts/ui/main_ui.gd, godot/scripts/ui/whats_new_modal.gd, godot/scripts/ui/welcome_screen.gd. See PLAYER_DOCS_PIPELINE_2026-07-20.md (PR #718). Size: S (Phase 1).

issues.title: Leaderboard: enable remote board + hardening minimums

milestone.title: v0.12 -- First Contact
milestone.description: Public alpha readiness (distribution channel decision owed -- see docs/ROADMAP.md): first-contact UX fixes, onboarding phases 0-2, export metadata, share loop, leaderboard hardening, monthly league v0. Capacity basis: ~1-2 effort-days/week.

issues.body: Shipped builds have data/leaderboard_config.json disabled with placeholder URL/token, so no shared board exists (premortem S1; UX teardown D). Enable against the real score_api.php endpoint and add the hardening minimums before strangers arrive: per-IP rate limit, display-name filter, per-(seed,version) boards (already in contract), and a published 'boards may be wiped/rolled during alpha' policy line. Note the client ships a shared token -- acceptable for alpha with rate limits, revisit with replay-verification as the real anti-cheat later. Cross-repo: pdoom1-website#133 (game->website score uplift broken post-Godot) is the same workstream. Size: S-M.

issues.title: Game-over share: copy result + seed one-liner

milestone.title: v0.12 -- First Contact
milestone.description: Public alpha readiness (distribution channel decision owed -- see docs/ROADMAP.md): first-contact UX fixes, onboarding phases 0-2, export metadata, share loop, leaderboard hardening, monthly league v0. Capacity basis: ~1-2 effort-days/week.

issues.body: From the success premortem (S1/S3 tell-a-friend loop): runs are deterministic per seed but nothing exposes 'beat my run'. Add one button on the game-over screen producing a single shareable line, e.g.: 'I survived 14 months at 62% doom on seed weekly-2026-w29 -- P(Doom)1 v0.11.0'. Clipboard only, no network. Pairs with the leaderboard-enable issue. Size: S.
